text: It put me in mind of those occasions when rock groups lose key personnel, like they lose the drummer and the guitar player and the songwriter, and they find two guys through the back page of the music papers, and then they go out as if nothing's happened, and their hits dry up, right? Like, what is this, and how how is this going to sustain itself, and and no one's really going to buy it? Did you have that sensation? There was a famous there was a famous gig that the Who did where Keith Moon was too off his face on drugs and couldn't basically play. It was in Las Vegas, and Roger Daltrey basically said, "Is there anyone in the audience or in here who can play the drums?" And they got someone to climb up on stage and try and play with the Who. Yeah, so it was a bit like, yeah, and apparently it wasn't a great gig.
